title: Dynamic Typing
subtitle: One box. Different values.
goal: Learn that a JavaScript variable can store values of different types.
explanation: This means the same variable can store values of different types.
At first a variable may hold text, then a number, then a boolean.
The type belongs to the value itself, not to the variable.
That is why JavaScript is called a dynamically typed language.

analogy.title: Analogy
analogy.text: Imagine a regular box. Today it holds a book, tomorrow a ball, and the day after a phone. The box stays the same — only its contents change.

remember: One variable can hold different data types.
The type belongs to the value, not the variable.

mistake.title: Common mistake
mistake.text: Beginners often think a variable must always store one data type. In JavaScript, that is not the case.

quiz.question: What does dynamic typing mean?
options: One variable can store different data types.
All variables must be of type Number only.
